stat.purpose: The large number inside a KPI card.

body.purpose: Table cells, form fields, general copy.

label.purpose: Table headers, stat labels, badges, uppercase eyebrows.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Resting cards and stat tiles in light theme.

shadows.name: ambient-dark
shadows.purpose: Resting cards and stat tiles in dark theme (deeper, since dark surfaces need more contrast to read elevation).

shadows.name: drawer
shadows.purpose: Client/deal detail drawer sliding in from the right.

shadows.name: side-panel
shadows.purpose: AI Co-pilot side panel sliding in from the right.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Centered dialogs (e.g. email edit modal); the deepest, omnidirectional shadow in the system.

motion.name: panel-spring
motion.purpose: Framer Motion spring for the AI Co-pilot panel sliding in from the right.

motion.name: card-ease
motion.purpose: Bouncy ease for hover lifts and staggered card entrances.

motion.name: stagger-delay
motion.purpose: Per-card delay for KPI grid fade-up entrance.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button (Save, Send, Generate email).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 10px 18px; border: none; border-radius: 9px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #ffffff; background: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--confirmed-green, #14735d); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action button (Cancel, Copy) paired next to a primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 9px 18px; border: 1px solid var(--border2, #ccc7b9); border-radius: 9px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text-secondary, #57534a); background: transparent; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); border-color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); background: rgba(15,110,86,0.08); }

components.name: KPI Stat Card
components.description: Dashboard KPI tile with label and large accent-colored value.
components.css: .ds-stat-card { background: var(--card, #faf8f2); border: 1px solid var(--border2, #ccc7b9); border-radius: 14px; padding: 22px 24px; box-shadow: 0 2px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); transition: transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-stat-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-stat-label { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: var(--text-secondary, #57534a); margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-stat-value { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 34px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); letter-spacing: -0.6px; line-height: 1; }

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: Toggleable filter chip (All / Critical / At Risk / Healthy).
components.css: .ds-filter-pill { padding: 8px 18px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd8cc); background: transparent; color: var(--text-secondary, #57534a); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-filter-pill--active { border-color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); background: rgba(15,110,86,0.10); color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Journey-stage indicator (Active/At-Risk/Critical/Expired). Uses fixed hex pairs per the Colors → Known inconsistency note; does not currently adapt to dark theme.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; padding: 3px 12px; border-radius: 6px; } .ds-status-chip--active { background: #d7e9e1; color: #1b6a58; } .ds-status-chip--at-risk { background: #efe4c4; color: #7d6217; } .ds-status-chip--critical { background: #f3dada; color: #a83838; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Primary navigation row inside the collapsible left rail.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; width: 100%; padding: 10px 12px; border: none; border-radius: 9px; background: transparent; color: var(--text-secondary, #57534a); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14.5px; font-weight: 500; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.12s ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--hover2, #dedacf); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: rgba(15,110,86,0.18); color: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); }

components.name: Insight Row (AI Co-pilot)
components.description: Compact alert/action row used inside the AI Co-pilot panel's Priority Actions and Deal Alerts sections; severity shown via a small dot rather than a full-color badge.
components.html:
Acme Corp — renewal at risk
No contact in 34 days, renews in 12.
components.css: .ds-insight-row { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd8cc); background: var(--bg, #ece8e0); } .ds-insight-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--amber-caution, #b8740f); flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 5px; } .ds-insight-title { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary, #2a2a28); margin-bottom: 2px; } .ds-insight-reason { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-tertiary, #827d6f); line-height: 1.4; }

components.name: Retention Area Chart
components.description: First recharts usage in the system (Analytics → Retention trend). Gradient-filled area over a horizontal-only grid, all styling driven by tokens instead of recharts defaults; this static SVG is a representative preview, not the live recharts output.
components.html: Retention trend
components.css: .ds-chart-card { background: var(--card, #faf8f2); border: 1px solid var(--border2, #ccc7b9); border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px 18px; } .ds-chart-title { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary, #2a2a28); margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-chart-svg { width: 100%; height: 120px; overflow: visible; } .ds-chart-grid { stroke: var(--border, #ddd8cc); stroke-width: 1; } .ds-chart-line { fill: none; stroke: var(--brand-bright, #6FD9B7); stroke-width: 2.5; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; } .ds-chart-dot { fill: var(--brand-bright, #6FD9B7); stroke: var(--card, #faf8f2); stroke-width: 2; }

components.name: Inline Log-Entry Form
components.description: "+ Log a call" / "+ Log a meeting" in the client drawer's Activity tab. Same inline bordered-box pattern as the drawer's existing "+ Add contact" form -- a short capture form stays inline in the flow rather than opening a modal.
components.html: Log a call
components.css: .ds-log-form { border: 1px solid var(--border2, #ccc7b9); border-radius: 10px; padding: 12px 13px; background: var(--hover, #e8e4da); } .ds-log-form-title { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary, #2a2a28); margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-log-form-field { width: 100%; padding: 8px 11px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd8cc); background: var(--bg, #ece8e0); color: var(--text-primary, #2a2a28); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13.5px; box-sizing: border-box; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-log-form-actions { display: flex; gap: 8px; } .ds-log-form-save { flex: 1; padding: 8px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; background: var(--signal-green, #0f6e56); color: #fff; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; } .ds-log-form-cancel { padding: 8px 14px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid var(--border2, #ccc7b9); background: transparent; color: var(--text-tertiary, #827d6f); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; cursor: pointer; }

components.name: Activity Row
components.description: Timeline row for a logged activity (call/meeting/email/deal move/contact/quote event). Circular type-colored icon on a connecting vertical line, title/description/meta stack.
components.html:
☎
Call logged
Discussed Q3 renewal terms, they want a 10% volume discount.
Marcus Chen4 min ago
components.css: .ds-activity-row { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; padding: 10px 8px; border-radius: 10px; } .ds-activity-icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--confirmed-green, #14735d); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 10px; color: #fff; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-activity-title { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary, #2a2a28); } .ds-activity-desc { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: var(--text-secondary, #57534a); margin-top: 2px; } .ds-activity-meta { display: flex; gap: 12px; margin-top: 4px; font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-tertiary, #827d6f); }

narrative.overview:
RetainIQ's interface is an instrument panel for catching account risk before it becomes churn, not a decorative CRM shell. Every recurring visual device — the pulsing live-status dot, the risk gauge, the color-coded journey stages — exists to answer one question at a glance: is this account fine, or does it need a call today. Signal Green is deliberately the resting/all-clear state; Amber and Red are reserved for escalating alarm, and that reservation is the entire point of the palette.

The voice is calm and clinical: dense data surfaces (KPI grids, sortable tables, drawers packed with contract detail) rendered with soft ambient shadows, restrained 8–16px radii, and a single typeface (Inter) carrying the whole hierarchy through weight and size rather than mixed fonts. Motion is present but purposeful — spring-eased panels, staggered card fade-ups, a pulsing dot on live status — never decorative for its own sake. The system supports full light/dark theming through CSS custom properties; almost every surface, text, and accent color routes through a var(--token) rather than a literal value.
keyCharacteristics: One typeface (Inter) for the entire hierarchy — no serif/mono pairing despite what older project docs claim.
Signal Green as the calm baseline; Amber/Red exist only to signal escalating risk.
Flat-by-default surfaces with soft ambient shadows at rest, deeper directional shadows on overlays.
Small, consistent radii (6–16px) plus a 999px pill for filters and status pills — no sharp corners, no heavy rounding.
Full light/dark theming via CSS custom properties, with a brighter accent variant (--brand-bright) swapped in for dark mode where the flat brand color would go flat against a dark surface.

rules.name: The Reserved Alarm Rule
rules.body: Amber and Red never appear as decoration or as a general accent — they exist exclusively to mark At-Risk and Critical/Expired states. If a new element needs an eye-catching color without implying risk, reach for Signal Green or AI Purple, never Amber/Red.

rules.name: The Bright Variant Rule
rules.body: Dark theme doesn't just invert lightness — where the flat brand accent (--cyan, #0f6e56) would read flat against a dark surface, dark mode swaps in a distinct brighter token (--brand-bright, #6FD9B7) for foregrounded uses like active nav text and the logo.

rules.name: The One Font Rule
rules.body: Every weight and size in the system comes from Inter. Do not introduce a second family for "editorial" or "technical" flavor — hierarchy is built with size, weight, and letter-spacing, not font mixing.

rules.name: The Directional Shadow Rule
rules.body: An overlay's shadow points toward the edge it emerged from, reinforcing where it came from and that it's temporary. A modal, which has no single edge of origin, gets an omnidirectional shadow instead.

rules.name: The No-Default-Recharts Rule
rules.body: Every recharts prop with a visual default (line color, grid, tooltip box, tick font) must be explicitly overridden with a system token before shipping, and every chart declares accessibilityLayer plus a descriptive aria-label. An unstyled recharts chart is an instant tell.

rules.name: The Precise Recency Rule
rules.body: "Time ago" displays must be computed from a precise timestamp (loggedAt/created_at), never from a day-only date field -- a day-only value can never honestly render "just now". Keep a separate user-editable date field only when backdating the event itself matters to the feature.

dos: Do route every color through the existing CSS custom-property tokens (var(--bg), var(--cyan), var(--text2), etc.) so new UI themes automatically between light and dark.
Do use --brand-bright instead of --cyan for any element foregrounded on a dark surface (active nav, logo mark), per the Bright Variant Rule.
Do reserve Amber/Red exclusively for At-Risk / Critical-Expired states, per the Reserved Alarm Rule.
Do give overlay surfaces (drawers, side panels, modals) a shadow directional to the edge they emerge from; give modals an omnidirectional shadow instead.
Do follow the inline-style + CSS-variable pattern already used in every pages/ and components/ file for new UI — that is the actual, active design system.
Do override every recharts default (line color, grid, tooltip, tick font) with a system token and set accessibilityLayer before shipping a chart, per Components → Charts (Recharts).
Do compute "time ago" displays from a precise timestamp (loggedAt/created_at), never a day-only date field, per the Precise Recency Rule.
donts: Don't add new component classes to App.css. It's a legacy stylesheet from an earlier iteration of the UI: no page or component references any of its .card / .btn / .stat-card / .terminal-panel classes (or most of its keyframes) anymore — only the :root/[data-theme="dark"] variable declarations and the spin keyframe are still live.
Don't introduce a second typeface. Inter carries the entire hierarchy; treat the README's "Space Mono • DM Sans" line as stale, not a spec.
Don't copy the hardcoded status-chip hex pairs for new status/severity UI — route new work through the token system instead of replicating that inconsistency.
Don't use Amber or Red as a general-purpose accent color; they carry risk meaning everywhere else in the product.
Don't color a routine CRUD action (add/edit/delete) Amber or Red just because it needs some accent — this app's Delete buttons are neutral (--text3); reserve Amber/Red for actual risk states.
